what_it_does: Declares a single AXIS dict named 'viewer' — the viewer/principal axis (who). id/namespace 'viewer', one discrete field 'expertise' (novice | pilot), value_source 'pending' because no operator-mode/settings state exists yet, and a desc framing it as the control-density seam against which expert knobs resolve.
observations: field 'expertise' has values novice | pilot, described as a render-family/density pick
value_source is 'pending' — explicitly noted that no operator-mode state is built yet, slot wired, value pending
desc lists principals: Tim, the RHM, a client (each translated-for)
docstring stresses knobs resolve against expertise rather than a hardcoded advanced panel
standouts: value_source 'pending' — the axis is deliberately wired ahead of its data source ('resolves the moment a source exists')
control density (novice->plain, pilot->full knob-row) is made an axis, not a UI toggle
conventions: same AXIS = {id, namespace, fields, value_source, desc} shape as sibling axes files
uses value_source 'pending' (sibling state.py/type.py use 'live') to mark an unwired source
notes: The 'who' axis: control density / expertise (novice|pilot) against which knobs resolve; wired but value_source is pending until operator-mode state exists.
questions: What component will eventually supply the operator-mode/expertise value?
How do the three named principals (Tim/RHM/client) map onto the two-value novice|pilot field?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — docstring says 'VIEWER/principal axis (who)' and the dict encodes expertise/control-density; it candidly states the value source is not yet built.
apparent_themes: control density as a resolvable axis, not a hardcoded panel
intention_signals: Trying to make audience/expertise drive how much control a surface exposes, declaratively, so the moment an operator-mode source exists the density resolves without new branching code.
novelty: An axis intentionally shipped with value_source 'pending' — a forward-wired slot awaiting its data source.
contribution: Contributes the viewer/principal axis declaration — the control-density seam keyed on expertise (novice|pilot) and the pending-source wiring pattern.
summary_for_embedding: A declarative AXIS dict 'viewer' encoding the principal/control-density dimension with a discrete 'expertise' field (novice|pilot) and value_source 'pending' until operator-mode state exists.
intention_for_embedding: To make viewer expertise the seam that resolves how much control a surface exposes, wired ahead of its data source.
